---
title: Dfs0 - Relative time axis
jupyter: python3
---


A dfs0 file can have either:

* **absolute time** (`pandas.DatetimeIndex`) - calendar timestamps
* **relative time** (`pandas.TimedeltaIndex`) - elapsed time from the start of the series

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import mikeio
```

```{python}
ds_rel = mikeio.read("../../data/eq_relative.dfs0")
type(ds_rel.time), ds_rel.time[:5]
```

```{python}
ds_abs = mikeio.read("../../data/da_diagnostic.dfs0")
type(ds_abs.time), ds_abs.time[:5]
```

Converting a relative axis to absolute timestamps is explicit: choose a reference start time and add the timedeltas.

```{python}
t0 = pd.Timestamp("2017-10-27 00:00:00")
t0 + ds_rel.time[:5]
```

The index type is preserved when converting to pandas:

```{python}
df_rel = ds_rel.to_dataframe()
type(df_rel.index), df_rel.head()
```
